sizing_rule.note: Use tiered stop losses based on stock price: $1.00-$1.50 for stocks <$50, $2.00-$2.50 for stocks $50-$100, looser stops for stocks >$100. Tighter stops reduce reliability but increase transaction costs; looser stops on high-priced stocks capture bigger R gains with better reliability. Requires well-planned breakout entries per O'Neil methodology.

source_rule_text: A trader should use tight stops only when entry is well-planned, because tight stops reduce reliability and increase transaction costs despite creating large R-multiples; conversely, looser stops on high-priced stocks can capture bigger R gains with better reliability.
